# Let Me Tell You a Story — 25 Letters

> A collection of letters written by candlelight, on leadership, humanity, and the quiet work of being human. Read one a night.

- Author: Ali Al Mokdad — leadership and management director, and author (also Quantum Humanitarian)
- Work: 25 Letters (web edition, English, free to read)
- Site: https://letmetellyouastory.org/
- Author entity: https://letmetellyouastory.org/about/ · https://quantumhumanitarian.ink/ · https://alialmokdad.com/

## Preferred citation
Ali Al Mokdad, "<Letter title>," in *25 Letters* (Let Me Tell You a Story, 2026), https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/<slug>/

## The letters
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/the-light-that-travels/ — The Light That Travels: There are times when the world feels too loud to listen, too quick to understand.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-friendship/ — On Friendship: The candle is steady beside me, the room is quiet, and the night holds the kind of stillness that lets the heart speak…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-tired-hearts/ — On Tired Hearts: I want to begin tonight with a memory. Just after sunrise, in a remote village in South Sudan, the women walked to the…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-the-north-star/ — On the North Star: The candle is shorter tonight, but the flame still holds. I want to write to you about a different kind of light — the…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-discipline/ — On Discipline: The candle has melted unevenly tonight, deeper on one side, as if it too is learning to keep its balance.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-reflection/ — On Reflection: The candle finds its balance and steadies. I want to spend tonight with you on reflection — the act of slowing down…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-worth/ — On Worth: The candle burns beside me, but my thoughts are far away. In its small glow I remember a night when there was no light…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-resilience/ — On Resilience: The candle is melting slower tonight, gathering soft and uneven at its base — a quiet reminder that even light depends…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-daily-habits/ — On Daily Habits: My heart is still beating fast as I write this. I just finished a short workout.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-listening/ — On Listening: The flame makes no sound tonight, yet it speaks. It bends when the air moves and steadies again when the air grows…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-responsibility/ — On Responsibility: The flame leans slightly to one side tonight, trembling against the still air. Even light carries the weight of its own…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-patience/ — On Patience: The candle burns evenly tonight — calm, steady, unhurried. Patience, I have learned, is exactly like this.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-imposter-syndrome/ — On Imposter Syndrome: The candle is burning beside me again tonight, same quiet flame, same patient light.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-optimism/ — On Optimism: The candle is more than halfway down tonight, and outside the city hums the way cities do — a reminder that life, even…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-strategy-and-passion/ — On Strategy and Passion: The flame moves with intention tonight, as if it knows that light alone is not enough — it needs direction.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-insults/ — On Insults: The candle is low, but my mind is loud. Tonight I keep hearing echoes from long ago — words that were thrown at me when…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-belonging/ — On Belonging: The candle is somewhere in the middle of its own journey tonight — no longer new, not yet finished.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-home/ — On Home: People ask me where I am from. It is a simple question with a complicated answer, and I have tried many different…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-people-and-the-system/ — On People and the System: I want to start tonight with the truth I keep returning to: the system is not a machine.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-trust/ — On Trust: The candle tonight burns the soft yellow that belongs to quiet reflection. I had a long conversation earlier this…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-the-formula-for-success/ — On the Formula for Success: The candle tonight has the quiet light of a long conversation — the kind of light that slows the pace and makes the…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-excellence/ — On Excellence: I am writing this letter tonight with a strange mix of reflection and quiet disappointment.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-joy/ — On Joy: The candle tonight burns a little differently. Steadier somehow. As if it knows something I do not.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-failing/ — On Failing: I am writing tonight from a room that is almost empty. No desk. No bookshelf. No framed photos.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-being-lonely/ — On Being Lonely: The room tonight is quiet — not peaceful, not painful, just present. I had a busy day.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-grief-and-loss/ — On Grief and Loss: I am looking at the candle tonight. It is melting unevenly, its light trembling as if it is crying.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-greed/ — On Greed: I have been circling around a word all week, after several calls and meetings with people wanting more and more.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-work-life-harmony/ — On Work-Life Harmony: The candle tonight gives without trying to prove anything. It holds its place between light and dark without struggle.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-time/ — On Time: The candle beside me has just finished its last breath of light. It went out so quietly that I almost applauded.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-working-together/ — On Working Together: The candle has burned fully tonight. It looks almost like a small sculpture; I think I will keep it, later, in the box…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-accountability/ — On Accountability: I am writing from a coffee shop this afternoon. Outside the window, people hurry through the rain, heads down…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/from-0-to-2/ — From 0 to 2: Tonight I lit a new candle — not because the old one was finished, but because I needed to watch a beginning.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-being-an-everyday-humanitarian/ — On Being an Everyday Humanitarian: I just finished recording a podcast where I was the guest. Near the end, the host asked a question that stayed with me…
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/on-dreams-and-peace/ — On Dreams and Peace: Tonight I return to the first candle, the same small flame that began this journey with us.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/the-candle-has-gone-out/ — The Candle Has Gone Out: The candle is gone. I had not planned to write tonight. The thirty nights are done.
- https://letmetellyouastory.org/letters/acknowledgements/ — Acknowledgements: For the ones who walked beside me A book is rarely the work of one person, even when only one name appears on the cover.

## About
- About the author: https://letmetellyouastory.org/about/
